.ddx/executions/20260415T042450-19120590/manifest.json:14:    "description": "Follow-up from Stage 2.4 opus review: the resolver pattern established in Stage 2.2 (node/projects) and extended in Stage 2.4 (documents/docGraph) has 3 load-bearing shape issues that should be fixed before Stage 2.5+ compounds them across 7 more resolver beads.

1. Stable-key cursors. Current: resolver_node.go defines encodeCursor(i int) / decodeCursor(s string) that base64-wrap "cursor:<slice-index>". Slice-index cursors break when the underlying collection reorders between pages. Fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads, documents, commits, sessions, workers (mutated or resorted per query). Replace with ID-based or (sort-key, ID)-based stable cursors. New file: cli/internal/server/graphql/cursors.go with encodeStableCursor(id string) / decodeStableCursor(s string).

2. Constructor-enforced non-nil state. Current: every resolver has "if r.State == nil { return }" or "if r.WorkingDir == empty { return }" early returns. As Stage 2 grows this becomes 50+ copy-pasted guards. Replace with a NewResolver(state StateProvider, workingDir string) *Resolver constructor that rejects nil/empty and returns an error, drop the runtime checks.

3. Snapshot struct vs direct domain types. Current inconsistency: resolver_node.go uses ProjectSnapshot / NodeStateSnapshot (adapter structs) while resolver_documents.go uses docgraph.Document directly. Pick ONE approach and migrate the other. Recommendation: direct domain types (simpler, less boilerplate). Migrate resolver_node.go to drop ProjectSnapshot.

Not in scope: the prefix-registry for node(id) dispatch (concern 5 from the Stage 2.3 note) — keep that for when bead/doc/worker prefixes are added. Not in scope: splitting StateProvider into sub-interfaces (concern 1) — decide that lazily when the interface actually grows.

File: cli/internal/server/graphql/{cursors.go,resolver.go,resolver_node.go,resolver_documents.go,state_graphql.go}",
.ddx/executions/20260415T042450-19120590/manifest.json:15:    "acceptance": "(1) New cli/internal/server/graphql/cursors.go with encodeStableCursor and decodeStableCursor helpers that encode/decode a stable string key (not slice index). (2) resolver_node.go and resolver_documents.go both use the new helpers; old encodeCursor(i int) is deleted. (3) NewResolver constructor exists, rejects nil State and empty WorkingDir with a clear error. All "if r.State == nil" / "if r.WorkingDir == empty" guards removed from individual resolvers. (4) ProjectSnapshot removed — resolver_node.go returns docgraph or server domain types directly, matching the pattern in resolver_documents.go. (5) All existing integration tests (TestGraphQLNodeInfo/TestGraphQLProjects/TestGraphQLNodeByProjectID/TestGraphQLDocuments/TestGraphQLDocGraph) still pass. (6) go build ./... clean.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:12:    "title": "checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)",
.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",
.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:14:    "acceptance": "1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.",

- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.

.ddx/executions/20260503T030952-1063db26/manifest.json:12:    "title": "checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)",
.ddx/executions/20260503T030952-1063db26/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",

== What is already in tree at base 7ef7a4b1 ==

The cumulative work from prior attempts (commits including 3102f0da, c334d94e, f912445f, and others tagged [ddx-23978824]) is merged and present:

AC 1 — Unified view + async load:

    - HarnessStatuses resolver returns rows with kind=HARNESS, lastCheckedAt, defaultForProfile
    - ProviderStatuses returns rows with kind=ENDPOINT, lastCheckedAt
    - First-paint reads from cachedProviderRows; refreshProviderStatuses fires async
    - Synchronous probe path bounded to 500ms via context timeout
  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/+page.svelte
    - Renders providerStatuses + harnessStatuses in unified table with kind column
    - defaultRouteStatus issued as a separate parallel query (does not block table)
    - 2.5s poll interval patches rows after first paint (within 5s AC budget)
    - data-testid="agent-endpoints-table", endpoint-row-{name}, endpoint-kind-{name}, endpoint-reachable-{name}

AC 2 — Usage + quota + sparkline:
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:480 (buildUsage)
    - Returns nil ProviderUsage when all counts are zero — no fabricated "0/0" rows
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:421 (buildSparkline)
    - 24-element hourly token totals, returns nil when <6 non-zero buckets
  cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_headers.go
    - ParseRateLimitHeaders for claude/codex header families (with header fixtures in tests)
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go (harnessRateLimitCache + RecordHarnessRateLimit)
    - Bridge from captured rate-limit signal -> ProviderQuota fields (verified by TestHarnessQuotaFromCapturedRateLimitSignal)

    - Quota ceiling rendered as red dashed overlay (data-testid ceiling-overlay-7d/30d), NOT trailing text
    - Projection callout (data-testid projection-callout) rendered only when ceiling+positive slope present
  cli/internal/server/graphql resolver providerTrend exists with bucketed server-side aggregation

== What is NOT in tree (and why no commit was made this pass) ==

1. RecordHarnessRateLimit is never called from a non-test code path.
   - The function and the cache that bridges parsed headers -> ProviderQuota are wired and unit-tested end-to-end via TestHarnessQuotaFromCapturedRateLimitSignal.
   - But no production call site invokes ParseRateLimitHeaders + RecordHarnessRateLimit after a real claude/codex invocation. grep -rn "RecordHarnessRateLimit\|ParseRateLimitHeaders" outside of resolver_providers.go and *_test.go returns nothing.
   - Why this is non-trivial: claude-code and codex are subprocess CLIs. They do NOT return HTTP-style headers from a Go http.Client whose Header map can be passed straight to ParseRateLimitHeaders. To wire this for real, one of:
       (a) the harness adapters must capture the upstream HTTP response headers from inside the subprocess (requires Claude Code / codex protocol changes — not in our control), OR
       (b) we add a stdout/stderr-format parser that scrapes rate-limit fields the harnesses emit on completion (Claude Code emits a JSON line with usage; codex prints a rate-limit summary), and feed those into a synthetic header map or a new structured input to a sibling RecordHarness* function.
   - Option (b) is the realistic path but it is a meaningful adapter change. Per the standing directive recorded in memory (multi-model plan review for non-trivial plans, plus the standing "raise architectural decisions to the user" directive for substrate choices), I should NOT silently pick (b) — the user should weigh in on whether to scrape stdout/stderr per-harness or wait for upstream protocol features.

2. agent-endpoints.e2e.ts still mocks providerTrend instead of fetching from a server-seeded fixture.
   - The Go-side equivalent (TestProviderTrendProjectionFromSeededUsageAndCeiling) seeds usage + a real rate-limit-derived ceiling and asserts the projection field, satisfying the AC's "seeded fixture proves projection text appears" intent at the resolver layer.
   - Replacing the Playwright mock requires standing up the real Go server inside the Playwright config with seeded session-index files, which is an e2e-infrastructure change rather than a feature change. Worth doing, but again should be planned, not improvised in this attempt.

== Recommendation for next iteration ==

Open a small follow-up bead specifically for "wire harness rate-limit capture from claude/codex subprocess output" with the user choosing between options (a) and (b) above. That keeps this bead's quota-display surface honest (it works the moment any caller records a signal), and isolates the adapter work behind its own scope.

The reviewer's repeated REVIEW:BLOCK with "Diff scope wrong" message indicates that the reviewer is being shown only the post-merge bookkeeping commits (.ddx/executions/.../result.json) instead of the actual implementation diffs. That is an execute-bead evidence-extraction problem, not an implementation problem — the implementation is in the tree. Re-extracting at the documented base..result_rev range as the prior block notes already instruct should let the next review see the work that's there.

// (LAYER 1) is no longer needed because resolver methods read WorkingDir
// from the request context rather than a struct field.
func (s *Server) serveGraphQL(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, workingDir string) {
	gqlServer := s.graphqlHandler()
	// Plumb the originating *http.Request into the resolver context so
	// resolvers (e.g. operatorPromptSubmit) can validate the CSRF header
	// and capture the originating identity from headers tsnetMiddleware
	// injects (X-Tailscale-User / X-Tailscale-Node). Also inject the
	// per-request workingDir so resolvers reading WorkingDir do so under
	// the scoped project (LAYER 2 / ddx-055e8d32) rather than the
	// resolver's startup default.
	ctx := r.Context()
	ctx = ddxgraphql.WithHTTPRequest(ctx, r)
	ctx = ddxgraphql.WithWorkingDir(ctx, workingDir)
	gqlServer.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
}

// graphqlHandler lazily builds and caches the gqlgen handler used by both
// /graphql and the scoped /api/projects/{project}/graphql route. The
// resolver's WorkingDir field is set to s.WorkingDir as the FALLBACK
// default; per-request scoping flows through context via WithWorkingDir
// (see serveGraphQL).
func (s *Server) graphqlHandler() http.Handler {
	s.gqlOnce.Do(func() {
		var fedProvider ddxgraphql.FederationProvider
		if s.hub != nil {
			fedProvider = newHubFederationProvider(s)
		}
		resolver := ddxgraphql.NewResolver()
		resolver.State = s.state
		resolver.WorkingDir = s.WorkingDir
		resolver.Workers = s.workers
		resolver.BeadBus = s.beadHub
		resolver.Actions = &workerDispatchAdapter{manager: s.workers}
		resolver.ExecLogs = &execLogAdapter{}
		resolver.CoordMetrics = &coordMetricsAdapter{reg: s.workers.LandCoordinators}
		resolver.CSRFTokens = s.csrfTokens
		resolver.OperatorPromptIdempotency = s.operatorPromptIdempotency
		resolver.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist = s.operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist
		resolver.PromptCapBytes = serverPromptCapBytes
		resolver.BuildSHA = serverBuildSHA()
		resolver.NodeID = s.state.Node.ID
		resolver.ExecuteLoopWaker = s.workers
		resolver.Federation = fedProvider
		resolver.ReportedWorkers = s.reportedWorkers
		gqlServer := handler.New(ddxgraphql.NewExecutableSchema(ddxgraphql.Config{
			Resolvers:  resolver,
			Directives: ddxgraphql.DirectiveRoot{},
		}))
		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.POST{})
		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.GET{})
		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.Websocket{
			Upgrader: websocket.Upgrader{
				CheckOrigin: func(r *http.Request) bool { return true },
			},
			KeepAlivePingInterval: 30 * time.Second,
		})
		s.gqlHandler = gqlServer
	})
	return s.gqlHandler
}

func (s *Server) handleGraphiQL(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	if r.Method != http.MethodGet {
		writeJSON(w, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed, map[string]string{"error": "method not allowed"})
		return
	}

	// Serve GraphiQL HTML page with inline GraphQL endpoint reference
	graphiqlHTML := `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
	<meta charset="utf-8">
	<title>GraphiQL - DDx</title>
	<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/graphiql@0.18.0/graphiql.min.css" />
	<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/subscriptions-transport-ws@0.11.0/browser/client.js"></script>

---
# DDx GraphQL Schema
# Single source of truth for the SvelteKit UI.
# Every type field is doc-commented. Relay cursor connections on all lists.

# ============================================
# Scalars
# ============================================

"""ISO-8601 datetime string"""
scalar DateTime

"""Arbitrary JSON blob (used for opaque map fields)"""
scalar JSON

# ============================================
# Interfaces
# ============================================

"""Node is the Relay global object identification interface"""
interface Node {
  """Globally unique identifier"""
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Pagination (Relay Cursor Connections)
# ============================================

"""PageInfo carries cursor-based pagination state (Relay spec)"""
type PageInfo {
  """True when more edges exist after the last returned edge"""
  hasNextPage: Boolean!
  """True when more edges exist before the first returned edge"""
  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
  """Cursor of the first edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  startCursor: String
  """Cursor of the last edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  endCursor: String
}

# ============================================
# Node & Project
# ============================================

"""NodeInfo represents the running ddx-server instance"""
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  """Unique node identifier (stable across restarts)"""
  id: ID!
  """Human-readable node name"""
  name: String!
  """Timestamp when the server process started"""
  startedAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

"""Project represents a registered ddx project on this node"""
type Project implements Node {
  """Unique project identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Short display name"""
  name: String!
  """Absolute filesystem path to the project root"""
  path: String!
  """Git remote URL, if configured"""
  gitRemote: String
  """When the project was first registered with the server"""
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the last successful probe"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  """True when the project path is inaccessible"""
  unreachable: Boolean
  """Set when the project has been tombstoned (soft-deleted)"""
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

"""ProjectEdge is one edge in a ProjectConnection"""
type ProjectEdge {
  """The project at this position"""
  node: Project!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""ProjectConnection is a Relay cursor connection of projects"""
type ProjectConnection {
  """Ordered list of project edges"""
  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of projects matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Beads
# ============================================

"""Bead is a portable work item with lifecycle metadata"""
type Bead implements Node {
  """Unique bead identifier (e.g. "ddx-a1b2c3d4")"""
  id: ID!
  """Short summary of the work"""
  title: String!
  """Lifecycle status: open, in-progress, closed, blocked"""
  status: String!
  """Numeric priority (lower is higher priority)"""
  priority: Int!
  """Issue classification (e.g. "task", "bug", "spike")"""
  issueType: String!
  """Agent or human currently responsible"""
  owner: String
  """When the bead was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime!
  """Who or what created the bead"""
  createdBy: String
  """Last modification timestamp"""
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  """Freeform labels for filtering and grouping"""
  labels: [String!]
  """Project identifier this bead belongs to"""
  projectID: String
  """Parent bead ID for hierarchy"""
  parent: String
  """Full description / body text"""
  description: String
  """Acceptance criteria text"""
  acceptance: String
  """Freeform notes appended during work"""
  notes: String
  """Dependency edges to other beads"""
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

"""Dependency is a directed edge between two beads"""
type Dependency {
  """The bead that has the dependency"""
  issueId: String!
  """The bead that must be completed first"""
  dependsOnId: String!
  """Dependency kind (e.g. "blocks", "relates-to")"""
  type: String!
  """When the dependency was created"""
  createdAt: String
  """Who created the dependency"""
  createdBy: String
  """Opaque metadata attached to the link"""
  metadata: String
}

"""BeadEdge is one edge in a BeadConnection"""
type BeadEdge {
  """The bead at this position"""
  node: Bead!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""BeadConnection is a Relay cursor connection of beads"""
type BeadConnection {
  """Ordered list of bead edges"""
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of beads matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

"""BeadStatusCounts holds aggregate bead counts by status"""
type BeadStatusCounts {
  """Number of beads in open status"""
  open: Int!
  """Number of beads in closed status"""
  closed: Int!
  """Number of beads with unclosed dependencies"""
  blocked: Int!
  """Number of open beads whose dependencies are all closed"""
  ready: Int!
  """Total number of beads"""
  total: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Documents (library)
# ============================================

"""Document represents a docgraph document with dependency tracking"""
type Document implements Node {
  """Document identifier (usually the relative path)"""
  id: ID!
  """Filesystem path relative to the library root"""
  path: String!
  """Human-readable title extracted from frontmatter"""
  title: String!
  """IDs of documents this document depends on"""
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  """IDs of input artifacts consumed by this document"""
  inputs: [String!]!
  """IDs of documents that depend on this document"""
  dependents: [String!]!
  """Review state metadata (null if never reviewed)"""
  review: DocumentReview
  """True if parked (excluded from staleness checks)"""
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  """Prompt text embedded in the document frontmatter"""
  prompt: String
  """Execution definition embedded in the document (null if none)"""
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
  """Raw file content (populated only by documentByPath, null in list queries)"""
  content: String
}

"""DocumentReview holds review-state metadata for a document"""
type DocumentReview {
  """Hash of the document content at review time"""
  selfHash: String!
  """Map of dependency IDs to their hashes at review time"""
  deps: JSON!
  """ISO-8601 timestamp of the last review"""
  reviewedAt: String!

---
     1	package graphql
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"fmt"
     6		"strings"
     7		"sync"
     8		"time"
     9	
    10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
    11		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
    12	)
    13	
    14	var providerStatusCache = struct {
    15		sync.Mutex
    16		rows     map[string][]*ProviderStatus
    17		inFlight map[string]bool
    18	}{
    19		rows:     make(map[string][]*ProviderStatus),
    20		inFlight: make(map[string]bool),
    21	}
    22	
    23	// harnessRateLimitCache holds the most recently observed rate-limit signal per
    24	// harness name. The server invocation path (or tests) populates it via
    25	// RecordHarnessRateLimit; quotaFromHarnessInfo reads it when the upstream
    26	// HarnessInfo.Quota does not carry token-level ceilings.
    27	var harnessRateLimitCache = struct {
    28		sync.RWMutex
    29		byName map[string]agent.RateLimitSignal
    30	}{byName: make(map[string]agent.RateLimitSignal)}
    31	
    32	// LookupHarnessRateLimit returns the last-observed signal for a harness, or
    33	// ok=false if none has been recorded.
    34	func LookupHarnessRateLimit(name string) (agent.RateLimitSignal, bool) {
    35		harnessRateLimitCache.RLock()
    36		defer harnessRateLimitCache.RUnlock()
    37		sig, ok := harnessRateLimitCache.byName[name]
    38		return sig, ok
    39	}
    40	
    41	// ProviderStatuses is the resolver for the providerStatuses field.
    42	// It mirrors the output of `ddx agent providers`, annotating each row with
    43	// kind=ENDPOINT, a lastCheckedAt timestamp, and rolling usage derived from
    44	// the sessions index. Quota is populated when the upstream ProviderInfo
    45	// exposes token-level quota headers; null otherwise (FEAT-014 no-fabrication).
    46	func (r *queryResolver) ProviderStatuses(ctx context.Context) ([]*ProviderStatus, error) {
    47		now := time.Now().UTC()
    48		entries := r.sessionIndexEntries(ctx)
    49	
    50		if rows := cachedProviderRows(r.workingDir(ctx)); len(rows) > 0 {
    51			refreshProviderStatuses(r.workingDir(ctx))
    52			return providerRowsWithUsage(rows, entries, now), nil
    53		}
    54	
    55		if snapshots, ok, err := agent.ConfiguredProviderSnapshots(r.workingDir(ctx)); err == nil && ok {
    56			rows := providerStatusesFromInfos(snapshots, entries, now)
    57			storeProviderRows(r.workingDir(ctx), rows)
    58			refreshProviderStatuses(r.workingDir(ctx))
    59			return rows, nil
    60		} else if err != nil {
    61			return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading provider snapshots: %w", err)
    62		}
    63	
    64		// Legacy/global provider config fallback. Bound the synchronous path so the
    65		// UI can still first-paint harness rows even if provider probing is slow.
    66		probeCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 500*time.Millisecond)
    67		defer cancel()
    68		providers, err := liveProviderInfos(probeCtx, r.workingDir(ctx))
    69		if err != nil {
    70			refreshProviderStatuses(r.workingDir(ctx))
    71			return []*ProviderStatus{}, nil
    72		}
    73		rows := providerStatusesFromInfos(providers, entries, now)
    74		storeProviderRows(r.workingDir(ctx), rows)
    75		return rows, nil
    76	}
    77	
    78	// HarnessStatuses is the resolver for the harnessStatuses field.
    79	// It returns one row per subprocess harness (kind=HARNESS). Reachability is
    80	// taken from HarnessInfo.Available, rolling usage from the sessions index,

---
   488	}

   490	// quotaFromProviderInfo derives a ProviderQuota from the upstream ProviderInfo.
   491	// Returns nil when no token-level ceiling is published.
   492	func quotaFromProviderInfo(p agentlib.ProviderInfo) *ProviderQuota {
   493		if p.Quota == nil {
   494			return nil
   495		}
   496		return quotaFromState(p.Quota)
   497	}
   498	
   499	// quotaFromHarnessInfo derives a ProviderQuota from the upstream HarnessInfo.
   500	// When a rate-limit signal has been captured for this harness (via
   501	// RecordHarnessRateLimit — typically on the server's harness-dispatch path
   502	// after parsing response headers with agent.ParseRateLimitHeaders), that
   503	// signal takes precedence because it carries absolute token ceilings. The
   504	// upstream HarnessInfo.Quota only exposes percent-used windows without an
   505	// absolute ceiling. Returns nil when nothing usable is published.
   506	func quotaFromHarnessInfo(info agentlib.HarnessInfo) *ProviderQuota {
   507		if sig, ok := LookupHarnessRateLimit(info.Name); ok {
   508			if q := QuotaFromRateLimitSignal(sig); q != nil {
   509				return q
   510			}
   511		}
   512		if info.Quota == nil {
   513			return nil
   514		}
